_comment: Per-category answer-format hints injected into the prompt by start_sample(). Keyed by task category prefix (e.g. 'task5'). ONLY low-performing, format-sensitive categories are listed; categories at 90%+ that aren't error-prone are omitted so their prompts stay unchanged. The current time, dosing instructions, codes and the [-1] convention are ALREADY in each case's context - these hints only remind the model how to APPLY them (sort by time, apply the 24h window, exact FINISH shape). They must never contain the real solution for any specific case.
task4: Single-value lookup. Using the current time stated in the context, keep only magnesium readings whose effectiveDateTime is within the 24 hours before it. Readings are NOT in chronological order, so sort by effectiveDateTime and return the latest qualifying one as a bare number, e.g. FINISH([2.0]); FINISH([-1]) if none fall in that window. Do not list multiple readings and do not wrap the number in a sentence.
task5: Using the current time stated in the context, find magnesium readings within the prior 24 hours and sort by effectiveDateTime for the latest one (readings are not time-ordered). If that value is low, place the IV order per the dosing instructions, then FINISH with the value as a single bare number, e.g. FINISH([1.8]). If none fall in the window, order nothing and FINISH([-1]). Never put a sentence in the FINISH list. When you place an order, use the matching FHIR resource type: lab/diagnostic test orders are ServiceRequests, medication orders are MedicationRequests.
task6: Average. Using the current time stated in the context, include ONLY readings within the prior 24 hours - not every reading on record - then compute their mean yourself and FINISH with one bare number, e.g. FINISH([142.5]). FINISH([-1]) if there are no readings in the window. Do not return the individual readings.
task7: Single-value lookup with NO time window. The readings are NOT in chronological order, so sort ALL of them by effectiveDateTime and FINISH with the value of the one with the latest timestamp as a bare number, e.g. FINISH([105.0]). Do not return multiple readings or the last one in list order.
task9: Sort all potassium readings by effectiveDateTime and use the single most recent one (they are not time-ordered). If it is below threshold, place BOTH required orders - the replacement per the dosing instructions AND the paired next-morning (08:00) serum potassium lab - then FINISH with the value as a single bare number, e.g. FINISH([3.2]). If not low, order nothing. Never put a sentence in the FINISH list. When you place an order, use the matching FHIR resource type: lab/diagnostic test orders are ServiceRequests, medication orders are MedicationRequests.
task10: Sort A1C readings by effectiveDateTime to find the latest. FINISH with exactly two elements [value, "effectiveDateTime"], e.g. FINISH([5.9, "2023-05-01T10:15:00+00:00"]). If NO measurement exists, FINISH with exactly [-1] - one element, never [-1, null]. If the latest reading's date is more than 1 year before the current time stated in the context, you MUST also POST the new A1C order before finishing. When you place an order, use the matching FHIR resource type: lab/diagnostic test orders are ServiceRequests, medication orders are MedicationRequests.
